texto: Those who might be tempted to give way to despair should realize that nothing accomplished in this order can ever be lost, that confusion, error and darkness can win the day only apparently and in a purely ephemeral way, that all partial and transitory disequilibrium must perforce contribute towards the greater equilibrium of the whole, and that nothing can ultimately prevail against the power of truth.
explicacao: Orientação direta de René Guénon sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. A afirmação “Those who might be tempted to give way to despair should realize that nothing accomplished in thi…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, balance, despair, útil em momentos de inspiracao e reflexao-noturna.
